fields.description:
## What
Persist a bounded bug-classification enum at close, replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`. Adds a REQUIRED `--class` param to `transition` for bug closes and folds `close_class` into reduced ticket state so it survives and is queryable, with the vocabulary that feeds the classification metrics.

## Files
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` — parse a new `--class <value>` flag in `_parse_flags` (alongside `--reason`).
- `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` — validate the enum for bug closes (replacing `bug_close_reason_ok` at the transition_core guard, ~line 221-232) and write `close_class` into `status_data`.
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` — CRITICAL: the `_completion_precheck` guard at line ~121 also calls `txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason)`; update it to accept a valid `close_class` (or skip the reason pre-check when `close_class` is supplied). Thread `close_class` through the close path.
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` — CRITICAL: `process_status` only folds a fixed set of keys; add a `_fold_close_class(state, data)` helper (mirroring `_fold_claimed_session`) that sets `state['close_class'] = data['close_class']` on a `*->closed` edge, called in BOTH the fork-winner and normal-update branches — otherwise `close_class` never surfaces into reduced state and `rebar show` cannot see it.
- `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json` — add optional `close_class`.
- `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt`, `docs/ticket-model.md`, `docs/user-guide.md` — document the enum.
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_bug_close_class.py` — new.

## Approach
The bug-close reason is required today but never persisted (`txn.py` validates then drops it), so replacing it with a bounded, folded enum is friction-neutral-to-negative and turns wasted input into a hard signal. `--class` is REQUIRED on bug close (mirrors the old required `--reason`): omitting it exits nonzero with a message listing the allowed values. Because `status_data` keys are not auto-surfaced, the enum only reaches `rebar show` via an explicit `_fold_close_class` fold — the exact-parallel of `_fold_claimed_session`. The guard lives at two sites (`txn.py` transition_core AND `transition_close.py` `_completion_precheck`), both updated. Closed MECE vocabulary with an escape + hygiene values: `regression`, `plan_defect`, `env_integration`, `flaky`, `preexisting`, `not_a_bug`, `duplicate`, `escalated`, `undetermined`. `escalated` preserves today's Escalated branch; the rest refine Fixed. Additive/optional schema field (readers tolerate absence); back-out = stop requiring/writing it.

## Tests
- CLI (real subprocess): closing a bug with `--class regression` persists and surfaces `close_class=regression`; omitting `--class` exits nonzero; an invalid class is rejected; `undetermined` is accepted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_bug_close_class.py` passes: `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --class regression` exits 0 and `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class==\"regression\"'` matches (proving the fold reaches reduced state).
- [ ] closing a bug with NO `--class` exits nonzero AND the captured stderr names at least one allowed value (asserts `exit != 0` AND `result.stderr` contains an enum value name, e.g. the string `regression`) — so the "message names the allowed values" UX claim is actually measured, not just the exit code.
- [ ] a value outside the enum is rejected with a nonzero exit — CLI test asserts exit != 0.
- [ ] closing with `--class undetermined` succeeds (escape value) — CLI test asserts exit 0.
